fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Kansas Licensed Dietitian (LD) Application — Requirements & Fees (2026)
seo.description: Apply for a Kansas Licensed Dietitian credential through KDADS. $140 application fee. Master's degree, 1,200 hours supervised practice, and CDR exam required.
seo.keywords: Kansas dietitian license, KS licensed dietitian requirements, KDADS Health Occupations Credentialing Dietitians, Kansas dietitian application fee

board.name: Health Occupations Credentialing
board.parent_dept: Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services (KDADS)

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The credential to practice dietetics in Kansas, regulated by the KDADS Health Occupations Credentialing division.

license_types.title: Temporary Licensed Dietitian
license_types.description: Available for applicants who have met all requirements except passing the exam. Valid for six months and can be renewed once. Temporary fee is $70.

steps.title: Complete Education & Supervised Practice
steps.description: Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,200 hours of board-approved supervised practice through an ACEND-accredited dietitian internship.

steps.title: Pass the CDR Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD status is required.

steps.title: Submit the Application to KDADS
steps.description: Download and complete the application form. Have it notarized, and mail it with the $140 fee to KDADS in Topeka.

steps.title: Provide Verification Documents
steps.description: Ensure your official transcripts are sent directly from the issuing institution. Include a copy of your CDR card as proof of examination and supervised training.

documents.detail: The original downloaded application form with a notarized signature.
A copy of your active CDR card to document exam and training completion.
Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.

provisional_text: Kansas offers a Temporary License for $70 for individuals who have fulfilled the educational and supervised practice requirements but have not yet passed the CDR exam. A temporary license allows the individual to practice under supervision for six months and may be renewed one time.
reciprocity_text: Kansas allows for licensure by reciprocity. Out-of-state applicants with a current, valid license from a state with equivalent or stricter standards, or holding active CDR registration, can apply using the standard application process to establish Kansas credentials.

faqs.question: How much does a Kansas dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial license application fee is $140. A temporary license costs $70.

faqs.question: When does the Kansas license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Kansas regular dietitian licenses are renewed biennially (every two years). The online renewal system opens annually on January 15th for those due. The renewal fee is $135.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements for Kansas?
faqs.answer: Kansas requires a minimum of 15 hours of Continuing Professional Education (CPE) every two years. Holding current CDR registration (which requires 75 CPEs every 5 years) generally satisfies this requirement.
